questions.question: Define controlled access and explain its importance in a computer network. (10 marks)

A multi-access network has a user station that transmits data at a rate of 10 Mbps and a channel capacity of 20 Mbps. If the network uses ALOHA II protocol, what is the probability that two or more users will transmit data at the same time? (8 marks)

An 802.11 wireless network operates in the DSSS mode with a data rate of 1 Mbps and a transmission power of 20 dBm. If the signal strength at the receiver is 10 dBm, what is the signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) of the received signal? (6 marks)
